title: Review: sieve-form 0.1.0
notes:
Idiom rating: ACCEPTABLE — the core design is solid and the architecture is idiomatic. The form layer and evaluator are well-structured. Main gaps: missing PartialEq derives on Form and SieveErrorKind hurt testability, SieveAction needs #[non_exhaustive] before 0.1.0 ships, ParseError needs impl Error. No unsafe, no gratuitous clones, no logic errors in the RFC-required behavior.
Confidence: 85% chance of missed issue. Least confident flags: the u64→usize truncation on 32-bit (may be intentional given 64-bit-only target assumption). Uncertainty driven by: (1) no explicit 32-bit CI target visible, (2) didn't run clippy live — may have additional lint hits in the Vec<char> / str_contains paths. Would update assessment if: Cargo.toml added explicit target constraints, or clippy --all-features output was reviewed.
